=============================================================================\n17\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n18\t// =============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n22\t// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n23\t//\n24\t// Parameters:\n25\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n157\t//\n158\t// Parameters:\n159\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n160\t//   - message: The core error message\n161\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n162\t//\n163\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n164\t//\n165\t// Example usage:\n166\t//\n167\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n169\t//\n170\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n180\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n181\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\t// Add field name if present\n185\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n203\t//\n204\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n205\t//\n206\t// Example usage:\n207\t//\n208\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n343\t//\n344\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n345\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n346\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n347\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n348\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// =============================================================================\n352\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n353\t// =============================================================================\n354\t\n355\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n356\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n357\t//\n358\t// Parameters:\n359\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n360\t//\n361\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n362\t//\n363\t// Example usage:\n364\t//\n365\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n366\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n370\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n371\t\tswitch category {\n372\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n395\t//\n396\t// Parameters:\n397\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n398\t//\n399\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n400\t//\n401\t// Example usage:\n402\t//\n403\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n404\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n453\t\tswitch severity {\n454\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n455\t\t\treturn \"!\"\n456\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n457\t\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n458\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n459\t\t\treturn \"■\"\n460\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n461\t\t\treturn \"○\"\n462\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n463\t\t\treturn \"i\"\n464\t\tdefault:\n465\t\t\treturn \"?\"\n466\t\t}\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t// =============================================================================\n470\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n471\t// =============================================================================\n472\t\n473\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n474\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n475\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n476\t//\n477\t// Parameters:\n478\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n479\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n480\t//\n481\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n482\t//\n483\t// Example usage:\n484\t//\n485\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n486\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n487\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n488\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n489\t//\t}\n490\t//\n491\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n492\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n493\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n494\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n495\t\n496\t\t// Add severity if requested\n497\t\tif includeSeverity {\n498\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n499\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n500\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n501\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n502\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n503\t\t\t} else {\n504\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n505\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n506\t\t\t}\n507\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\t// Add error type and field\n511\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n512\t\n513\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n514\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n515\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n516\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n517\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n518\t\t\t}\n519\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n520\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n521\t\t\t}\n522\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n523\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n524\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t// Add location if present\n529\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n530\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\treturn builder.String()\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n537\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n538\t//\n539\t// Parameters:\n540\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n541\t//\n542\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n543\t//\n544\t// Example usage:\n545\t//\n546\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n547\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n548\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n549\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n550\t//\t}\n551\t//\n552\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n553\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n554\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n555\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n556\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n557\t\t}\n558\t\n559\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t// =============================================================================\n563\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n564\t// =============================================================================\n565\t\n566\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n567\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n568\t//\n569\t// Parameters:\n570\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n571\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n572\t//\n573\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n574\t//\n575\t// Example usage:\n576\t//\n577\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n578\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n579\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n580\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n581\t//\t}, {\n582\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n583\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n584\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n585\t//\t}}\n586\t//\n587\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n588\t//\t// Returns:\n589\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n590\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n591\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n592\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n593\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\tvar lines []string\n597\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n598\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n599\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n600\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n607\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n608\t//\n609\t// Parameters:\n610\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n611\t//\n612\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n613\t//\n614\t// Example usage:\n615\t//\n616\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n617\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n618\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n619\t//\t}, {\n620\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n621\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n622\t//\t}}\n623\t//\n624\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n625\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n626\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n627\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n628\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n629\t\t}\n630\t\n631\t\t// Count errors by type\n632\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n633\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n634\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\t// Build summary parts\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\n643\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\t// =============================================================================\n647\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n648\t// =============================================================================\n649\t\n650\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n651\t// without the parent FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n681\t}\n682\t\n683\t// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n684\t// This provides a more explicit name for converting a Returns a formatted error message string.\n691\tfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n692\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n693\t}\n694\t\n695\t// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n696\t//\n697\t// Parameters:\n698\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n699\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n700\t//\n701\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n702\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n703\t\treturn instead of quotes.\n712\t//\n713\t// Parameters:\n714\t//   - value: The value to quote\n715\t//\n716\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n717\t//\n718\t// Example usage:\n719\t//\n720\t//\tquoted := \"\" {\n727\t\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n728\t\t}\n729\t\n730\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n734\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n735\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n736\t//\n737\t// Parameters:\n738\t//   - usage:\n744\t//\n745\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n746\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n747\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n748\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n749\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n750\t\t}\n751\t\n752\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n753\t\t\treturn value\n754\t\t}\n755\t\n756\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n757\t}\n758\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bf3db8ae-6d0a-46f8-958a-44c1ddab05b6","uuid":"89812c82-8f06-4884-8d2b-ff0a0274472f","timestamp":"2026-07-14T15:10:16.005Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_formatting.go","content":"package validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"!\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"■\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"○\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"i\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"?\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n","numLines":758,"startLine":1,"totalLines":758}}}

 \"user.address.city\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"whitespace handling\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"  email  \",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"  request  \",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"request.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index in field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index without parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index with bracket notation\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"data\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"data.users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"multiple array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"data.0.items.1.name\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.data[0].items[1].name\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"parent with array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"both parent and field with array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"profile.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].profile.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index at start of field\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"0.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty field name with array parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0]\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"deeply nested with array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"items.0.data.values.1.field\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.items[0].data.values[1].field\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"negative array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.-1.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.users[-1].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"large array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.12345.name\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[12345].name\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldRef(tt.fieldName, tt.parent)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldRef() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatFieldLocationWith(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname validate\n\n/*\nErrorType enum - Common validation error type categories\n\nThis file defines the ErrorType enum, which represents fundamental validation\nerror categories that can be used across different validation contexts. Unlike\nValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\ngeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n\nCommon ErrorType values include:\n  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n//\n// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n// error type specification. When you need to use these as strings, use\n// string(ErrTypeRequired) or ErrTypeRequired.String().\n//\n// Usage example:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message:   \"Field 'email' is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\ntype ErrorType string\n\n// Common validation error type enum values.\nconst (\n\t// ErrTypeRequired indicates a required field is missing or empty.\n\t// Use when a value must be present but is not provided.\n\t// Example: \"email field is required\"\n\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeRange indicates a value is outside acceptable numeric range.\n\t// Use when a numeric value is less than minimum or greater than maximum.\n\t// Example: \"age must be between 0 and 120\"\n\tErrTypeRange ErrorType = \"range\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeLength indicates string length or collection size is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value is too short or too long.\n\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is incorrect.\n\t// Use when a value is not of the expected type.\n\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific reasons.\n\t// Use when a value is present and correctly formatted but still invalid.\n\t// Example: \"country code 'XX' is not recognized\"\n\tErrTypeValue ErrorType = \"value\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeDuplicate indicates a duplicate value was detected.\n\t// Use when a value must be unique but already exists.\n\t// Example: \"email 'user@example.com' already exists\"\n\tErrTypeDuplicate ErrorType = \"duplicate\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeConflict indicates a conflict with existing values or constraints.\n\t// Use when a value conflicts with business logic or other values.\n\t// Example: \"start date cannot be after end date\"\n\tErrTypeConflict ErrorType = \"conflict\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeUnknown indicates an unknown error type.\n\t// This is the default/fallback for unrecognized error types.\n\tErrTypeUnknown ErrorType = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE METHODS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ErrorType.\n// This implements the Stringer interface.\nfunc (et ErrorType) String() string {\n\treturn string(et)\n}\n\n// IsValid returns true if this is a valid ErrorType constant.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsValid() bool {\n\tswitch et {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat, ErrTypeRange, ErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType, ErrTypeValue, ErrTypeDuplicate, ErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn true\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of this error type.\n// Returns \"Unknown error type\" if the type is not recognized.\nfunc (et ErrorType) Description() string {\n\tswitch et {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required field is missing or empty\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Value format is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Value is outside acceptable range\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"String length or collection size is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Value type is incorrect\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Value is invalid\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate value detected\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict with existing values or constraints\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown error type\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsRequired returns true if this is ErrTypeRequired.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsRequired() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeRequired\n}\n\n// IsFormat returns true if this is ErrTypeFormat.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsFormat() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeFormat\n}\n\n// IsRange returns true if this is ErrTypeRange.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsRange() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeRange\n}\n\n// IsLength returns true if this is ErrTypeLength.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsLength() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeLength\n}\n\n// IsType returns true if this is ErrTypeType.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsType() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeType\n}\n\n// IsValue returns true if this is ErrTypeValue.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsValue() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeValue\n}\n\n// IsDuplicate returns true if this is ErrTypeDuplicate.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsDuplicate() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeDuplicate\n}\n\n// IsConflict returns true if this is ErrTypeConflict.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsConflict() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeConflict\n}\n\n// IsUnknown returns true if this is ErrTypeUnknown.\nfunc (et ErrorType) IsUnknown() bool {\n\treturn et == ErrTypeUnknown\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE CONSTRUCTORS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorTypeFromString creates an ErrorType from a string.\n// Returns ErrTypeUnknown if the string doesn't match any known type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: ErrTypeRequired\n//\n//\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(\"invalid_type\")\n//\t// Returns: ErrTypeUnknown\nfunc ErrorTypeFromString(s string) ErrorType {\n\t// Check for exact match first (case-sensitive)\n\tswitch ErrorType(s) {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat, ErrTypeRange, ErrTypeLength,\n\t\tErrTypeType, ErrTypeValue, ErrTypeDuplicate, ErrTypeConflict,\n\t\tErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn ErrorType(s)\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// Check for case-insensitive match\n\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(s)\n\t\tswitch lower {\n\t\tcase \"required\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeRequired\n\t\tcase \"format\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeFormat\n\t\tcase \"range\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeRange\n\t\tcase \"length\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeLength\n\t\tcase \"type\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeType\n\t\tcase \"value\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeValue\n\t\tcase \"duplicate\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeDuplicate\n\t\tcase \"conflict\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeConflict\n\t\tcase \"unknown\":\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeUnknown\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn ErrTypeUnknown\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// MustParseErrorType creates an ErrorType from a string.\n// Panics if the string doesn't match any known type.\n//\n// This function is intended for use in initialization code where error types\n// are expected to be constant and correct.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrorType := MustParseErrorType(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: ErrTypeRequired\n//\n//\t// This will panic:\n//\terrorType := MustParseErrorType(\"invalid_type\")\nfunc MustParseErrorType(s string) ErrorType {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(s)\n\tif et == ErrTypeUnknown && s != \"unknown\" {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid error type: %s\", s))\n\t}\n\treturn et\n}\n\n// ErrorTypeList represents a collection of ErrorType values.\ntype ErrorTypeList []ErrorType\n\n// AllErrorTypes contains all defined error types.\nvar AllErrorTypes = ErrorTypeList{\n\tErrTypeRequired,\n\tErrTypeFormat,\n\tErrTypeRange,\n\tErrTypeLength,\n\tErrTypeType,\n\tErrTypeValue,\n\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\tErrTypeConflict,\n\tErrTypeUnknown,\n}\n\n// StructuralErrorTypes contains error types related to data structure validation.\nvar StructuralErrorTypes = ErrorTypeList{\n\tErrTypeRequired,\n\tErrTypeType,\n\tErrTypeLength,\n}\n\n// SemanticErrorTypes contains error types related to data meaning validation.\nvar SemanticErrorTypes = ErrorTypeList{\n\tErrTypeFormat,\n\tErrTypeRange,\n\tErrTypeValue,\n}\n\n// ConstraintErrorTypes contains error types related to constraint violations.\nvar ConstraintErrorTypes = ErrorTypeList{\n\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\tErrTypeConflict,\n}\n\n// true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// Strings returns a slice of string representations of the error types.\nfunc (etl ErrorTypeList) Strings() []string {\n\tresult := make([]string, len(etl))\n\tfor i, t := range etl {\n\t\tresult[i] = t.String()\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// Validate validates that this is a known error type.\n// Returns nil if valid, or an error if this is ErrorTypeUnknown (and not explicitly set).\nfunc (et ErrorType) Validate() error {\n\tif !et.IsValid() {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid error type: %s\", et)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// OrDefault returns this error type if valid, or ErrTypeUnknown if not.\nfunc (et ErrorType) OrDefault() ErrorType {\n\tif et.IsValid() {\n\t\treturn et\n\t}\n\treturn ErrTypeUnknown\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// IsValidBasicErrorType checks if a string is a valid basic ErrorType enum value.\n// This function works with the ErrorType enum (common validation errors),\n// not the string-based ErrorType constants used for HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif IsValidBasicErrorType(\"required\") {\n//\t    // Use the error type\n//\t}\nfunc IsValidBasicErrorType(s string) bool {\n\tet := len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n462\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n463\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn result\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// =============================================================================\n487\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n488\t// =============================================================================\n489\t\n490\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n491\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n492\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n493\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n494\t//\n495\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n496\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n497\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n498\t//\n499\t// Error Type Validation:\n500\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n501\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n502\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n503\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n504\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n505\t//\n506\t// Parameters:\n507\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n508\t//   - message: The error message content\n509\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n510\t//\n511\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n512\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n513\t//\n514\t// Example usage:\n515\t//\n516\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n517\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n518\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n519\t//\n520\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n521\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n522\t//\n523\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n524\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n525\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n526\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n527\t//\n528\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n529\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n530\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n531\t//\n532\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n533\t//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\n537\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n538\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n539\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n540\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n541\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n542\t\n543\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n544\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n545\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n546\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n547\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n548\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n551\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n552\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n553\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n557\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n558\t\n559\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n560\t\tif message == \"\" {\n561\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n562\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n563\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n564\t\t\t} else {\n565\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n566\t\t\t}\n567\t\t}\n568\t\n569\t\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\n570\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n571\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n572\t\t}\n573\t\n574\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\n575\t\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\n576\t\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\n577\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n581\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n582\t// instead of string-based error types.\n583\t//\n584\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n585\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n586\t//\n587\t// Parameters:\n588\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n589\t//   - message: The error message content\n590\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n591\t//\n592\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n593\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n594\t//\n595\t// Example usage:\n596\t//\n597\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\")\n601\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n602\t//\n603\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n604\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n605\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n606\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// =============================================================================\n627\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n628\t// =============================================================================\n629\t\n630\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n631\ttype QuoteStyle string\n632\t\n633\tconst (\n634\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n635\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n636\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n637\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n638\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n639\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n640\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes {\n646\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n647\t\tprefix     string\n648\t}\n649\t\n650\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n651\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n652\t\n653\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n654\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n655\t//\n656\t// Example usage:\n657\t//\n658\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n659\t//\t// Returns: WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n664\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n665\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n666\t\t}\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n670\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n671\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n672\t//\n673\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n674\t//\n675\t// Example usage:\n676\t//\n677\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n678\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n679\t//\n680\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", =============================================================================\n692\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n693\t// =============================================================================\n694\t\n695\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n696\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n697\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n698\t//\n699\t// Parameters:\n700\t//   - fieldPath: The field paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n704\t//\n705\t// Example usage:\n706\t//\n707\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n708\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n709\t//\n710\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n711\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n712\t//\n713\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n714\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n715\t//\n716\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n717\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n718\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n719\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n720\t\n721\t\t// Handle empty path\n722\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n723\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n724\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t// Parse and normalize the field \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n732\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n733\t\tif path == \"\" {\n734\t\t\treturn \"\"\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n738\t\tvar result []string\n739\t\n740\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n741\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n742\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n743\t\t\t\tcontinue\n744\t\t\t}\n745\t\n746\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n747\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n748\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n749\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n750\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n751\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n752\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n753\t\t\t\t} else {\n754\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n755\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n756\t\t\t\t}\n757\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n758\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n759\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n760\t\t\t} else {\n761\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n762\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n763\t\t\t}\n764\t\t}\n765\t\n766\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n767\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n771\t}\n772\t\n773\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n774\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n775\t\tif s == \"\" {\n776\t\t\treturn false\n777\t\t}\n778\t\n779\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n780\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n781\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n782\t\t\t\treturn false\n783\t\t\t}\n784\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n785\t\t}\n786\t\n787\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n788\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n789\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n790\t\t\t\treturn false\n791\t\t\t}\n792\t\t}\n793\t\n794\t\treturn true\n795\t}\n796\t\n797\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n798\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n799\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n800\t}\n801\t\n802\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n803\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n804\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n805\t// for field names through variadic options.\n806\t//\n807\t// Parameters:\n808\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n811\t//\n812\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n813\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n814\t//\n815\t// Example usage:\n816\t//\n817\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n818\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n819\t//\n820\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n821\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n822\t//\n823\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\"}\n849\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n850\t\t\topt(config)\n851\t\t}\n852\t\n853\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n854\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" QuoteStyle) string {\n906\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n907\t\t\treturn path\n908\t\t}\n909\t\n910\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n911\t\tvar fieldName name, add quoted version\n920\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 = true\n938\t\n939\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n940\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n941\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n942\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n943\t\n944\t\t\tdefault:\n945\t\t\t\t// Only validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n)\n\n// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n// It wraps the existing FormatValidationErrorWithDetails functionality with a more ergonomic interface.\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual         interface{}\n\tcontext        string\n\tresponseSnippet string\n\tfieldName      string\n\tpatternDetails string\n\trangeInfo      string\n\tvalidationDetails []string\n\tcustomSuggestions \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tformatter := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\nfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.expected = expected\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.actual = actual\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n// Context can include information like the endpoint being tested, operation type, etc.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.context = context\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithFieldName sets the specific field name
